content.problemStatement:
The Chessboard Distance for any two points (X1,Y1)(X_1, Y_1)(X1​,Y1​) and (X2,Y2)(X_2, Y_2)(X2​,Y2​) on a Cartesian plane is defined as max(∣X1−X2∣,∣Y1−Y2∣)max(|X_1 - X_2|, |Y_1 - Y_2|)max(∣X1​−X2​∣,∣Y1​−Y2​∣).

You are given two points (X1,Y1)(X_1, Y_1)(X1​,Y1​) and (X2,Y2)(X_2, Y_2)(X2​,Y2​). Output their Chessboard Distance.

Note that, ∣P∣|P|∣P∣ denotes the absolute value of integer PPP. For example, ∣−4∣=4|-4| = 4∣−4∣=4 and ∣7∣=7|7| = 7∣7∣=7.


Input Format

First line will contain TTT, the number of test cases. Then the test cases follow.
Each test case consists of a single line of input containing 444 space separated integers - X1,Y1,X2,Y2X_1, Y_1, X_2, Y_2X1​,Y1​,X2​,Y2​ - as defined in the problem statement.


Output Format
For each test case, output in a single line the chessboard distance between (X1,Y1)(X_1, Y_1)(X1​,Y1​) and (X2,Y2)(X_2, Y_2)(X2​,Y2​)
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains four space‑separated integers X1 Y1 X2 Y2.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line containing the Chessboard Distance between the two points, i.e. max(|X1‑X2|, |Y1‑Y2|).

sampleTestCases.explanation: First case: max(|2‑5|,|4‑1|)=max(3,3)=3.\nSecond case: max(|5‑5|,|5‑3|)=max(0,2)=2.\nThird case: max(|1‑3|,|4‑3|)=max(2,1)=2.
First case: differences are 1 and 1, distance = 1.\nSecond case: |10‑3|=7, |5‑20|=15, distance = max(7,15)=15.

solution.approach: For each test case we directly compute the absolute differences dx = |X1‑X2| and dy = |Y1‑Y2| using the built‑in absolute value function. The answer is the larger of the two values, max(dx, dy). This is O(1) per test case.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) – constant work for each of the T test cases.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only a few integer variables are used.

code.Python3:
#!/usr/bin/env python3
import sys

def main():
    data = sys.stdin.read().strip().split()
    if not data:
        return
    it = iter(data)
    T = int(next(it))
    out = []
    for _ in range(T):
        x1 = int(next(it)); y1 = int(next(it)); x2 = int(next(it)); y2 = int(next(it))
        dx = abs(x1 - x2)
        dy = abs(y1 - y2)
        out.append(str(max(dx, dy)))
    sys.stdout.write('\n'.join(out))

if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()
